observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the:
a) Property/Supply Officer –
i. Prepare a comparison list showing (1) asset items recorded in the
Municipal Accounting Unit but not included in the CDRA Inventory
Report, and (2) asset items appearing in the CDRA report but not in the
accounting records; and,
ii. Turn-over the comparison list and CDRA Inventory Report to the
Inventory Committee.
b) Inventory Committee -
i. Reperform the physical count of PPEs with reference to the comparison
list and CDRA report, using the ICF form to document the activity, and
clearly indicating in the ‘remarks’ column of the form, the items ‘found
at station’ or ‘missing/non-existing’, and other significant information
regarding the issue of ownership obtained from interviews/inquiries
conducted during the physical count activity.
ii. Assign unit values only to the items which LGU ownership had been
established, using information based on property/accounting records,
or in their absence, using the cost of a similar item counted or through
appraisal.
iii. Prepare the RPCPPE to summarize the entries in the ICF and provide
copies to the Municipal Accountant, COA & Property/Supply Officer.
c) Municipal Accountant –
i. Undertake collaboration with the Property/Supply Office to start the
reconciliation of the property records.
d) Municipal Mayor –
i. Mandate the conduct of an investigation for the items marked in the ICF
as ‘missing/non-existing’ during the physical count to determine their
last known location or place where installed, circumstances of the loss,
and the person accountable/responsible for the loss.
ii. File a request for authority to derecognize missing/non-existing PPEs
with the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor after
completion of the investigation.

2.18. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Order mandating the -
a) Municipal Engineer to:
i. Continue the inspection and inventory of the road networks, with the
assistance of the Municipal Assessor, General Services Officer
(GSO) and Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO),
making sure to segregate each road asset component, with
information as to its location and actual condition.
ii. Prepare the inventory report, adding therein the assessed values
provided by the Municipal Assessor, and copy furnish the COA, the
Municipal Accountant and the GSO for purposes of reconciliation
of records.
iii. Start obtaining information related to determining the ownership of
the roads and bridges in the submitted inventory derived from GPS
and QGIS.
b) Municipal Assessor to:
i. Assist the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer during the
conduct of inspection/inventory of the road lot components of all
road networks within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.
ii. Assess the cost of the road network/lot components, assigning such
values to the items identified during the inventory as owned and
controlled by the municipality.
iii. Provide the Municipal Engineer with the assessed values on the
road network/lot components identified, for the preparation of the
inventory report.
c) General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network
Property Cards based on the details obtained from the inventory report
furnished by the Municipal Engineer.

2.19. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Start the reconciliation of the records based on the inventory report
furnished by the Municipal Engineer.
b) Maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and subsidiary
ledgers with complete description of each road asset components, and
disclose such information in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years,
pursuant to the requirements under the details prescribed in COA
Circular Nos. 2015-008 and 2015-009.

2.20. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant,
General Services Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development
Officer explain the discrepancies mentioned in paragraph 2.10 of this
memorandum.

3.7. We recommended that the Inventory and Appraisal Committee submit to the
Municipal Accountant a copy of the Appraisal and Waste Material Report for
the Old Public Market. Henceforth, an Appraisal and Waste Material
Report shall always be prepared and a copy thereof submitted to the
Accounting Unit as supporting document to derecognize assets that have been
disposed by the municipality.

4.13. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the Status Report for the implementation of
infrastructure projects supported with the Certificate of Completion, SWA,
and Certificate of Acceptance and Turn-over immediately after its completion
on or before the closing of the year to facilitate recording of the asset accounts
in the accounting records.

4.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entry:
a. to record the completed projects totaling ₱9,806,674.70 in the books of
accounts. Henceforth, he shall set up at year-end the Accounts
Payable account along with recognizing the assets amounts for all
completed and accepted projects of the LGU, based on the Status
Report and other supporting documents submitted by the Municipal
Engineer;
b. to recognize in the books of the municipality the infrastructure projects
totaling ₱17,433,512.14, pending the preparation of documents showing
that an actual turnover of these projects had been made to the
barangays, until submission by the recipient barangay of the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) showing that the asset received from the
municipality had already been recorded in their books.

4.15. We also recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer facilitate
the transfer of completed projects by preparing the formal turnover
documents duly acknowledged by the recipient barangays.

5.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant discontinue the assignment of
residual values to Infrastructure Assets when computing for its depreciation
expenses, pursuant to the guideline established under DPWH DO 176.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry
Voucher to adjust the affected accounts for the errors noted in this
observation. Henceforth, he shall compute the depreciation of assets under
the Road Networks account using the straight line method, without allocating
a residual value for its depreciable components.

7.9 We recommended the Municipal Accountant:
a) review and validate past records to account the details of the Due to GSIS
account from the time the Subsidiary Ledgers were set up;
b) conduct a reconciliation between the confirmed balance and the accounting
records, taking note of variances between monthly withheld and remitted
amounts for premium contributions and loan amortization payments; and,
c) upon completion of the reconciliation, prepare the necessary adjusting
entry to correct the negative credit balances.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) request for confirmation from PhilHealth on the existence of any
unpaid balances, if any, due them by the Municipality of Jaro;
b.) conduct a reconciliation between the confirmed balance and the
accounting records, taking note of variances between monthly withheld
and remitted amounts; and,
c.) upon completion of the reconciliation, prepare the necessary adjusting
entry to close remaining dormant balances, if any, to the surplus of the
general fund

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant:
1. Record immediately all book reconciling items which were already identified in the previous Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS);
2. Prepare MONTHLY the BRS based on bank snapshots or bank statements obtained through We-Access of the LBP;
3. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to determine the transactions covered by the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers in CY 2022 and reconstruct all documents for submission to the Audit Team; and
4. Provide a cut-off date in the processing of December transactions, and inform all LGU personnel of such cut-off date, to completely account and record all transactions of the LGU in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the
Municipal Treasurer:
5. Make a formal inquiry with LBP management regarding the bank account which was not confirmed and identified in their reply to the Confirmation Letter.
6. Provide the Municipal Accountant with proof of closure of the PNB account as basis of the adjustment in the LGU books;
7. Notify in writing the payees of the stale checks and require them to return the check for issuance of a new one or waive their claims in favor of the LGU; and
8. Prepare a report of the cancelled checks on the period of cancellation and on the replacement checks and submit the same to the Audit Team.
9. Henceforth, submit all paid Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accountant on the prescribed due date, especially December transactions, for the timely recording in the books of the LGU.

inform all LGU personnel of such cut-off date, to completely account and
record all transactions of the LGU in the books of accounts.
1.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

account which was not confirmed and identified in their reply to the
Confirmation Letter.
b) Provide the Municipal Accountant with proof of closure of the PNB
account as basis of the adjustment in the LGU books;

Accountant.

which are listed in the accounting records as on-going.

carried in the Construction in Progress accounts based on the Status
Report of the Municipal Engineer and the accounting records on projects
with final payments;
b. Evaluate the dormant amounts in the Construction in Progress accounts
based on the nature of the transaction and on the Status Report of the
Municipal Engineer and prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the
CIP to either an asset or an expense;
c. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the negative balance in the
CIP account;
d. Henceforth, transfer the cost of completed projects, from Construction
in Progress account, to the appropriate asset account immediately upon
payment of final billing to the contractor. At the same time, record the
corresponding depreciation expense on the related PPE account.

3.12. We recommended that the Supply Officer prepare the Summary of Supplies
and Materials Issued (SSMI duly supported with pre-numbered RIS issued
for the supplies and materials issued totaling ₱5,379,402.35; and henceforth
submit the same to the Municipal Accountant every 1st week of the month for
every supplies and materials issued to end-users.

to record the expenses and reduce the Inventory account balances on the
issued inventories totaling ₱5,379,402.35 based on the SSMI and RIS prepared
by the Supply Officer; and, henceforth, record small tangible items with
estimated useful life of more than one year costing below P50,000.00 as
inventories upon acquisition and as expense upon issuance.

recommendations as discussed during the exit conference.

Accountant to prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books
for the above-mentioned transactions, and to record in the Inventory account all
supplies and materials upon its purchase and the related expenses when the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued are submitted by the end-users.

2.10 We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries both in the General and Trust
Fund books for details of transactions affecting the transfer and utilization of
DRRM fund accounts, in order to depict correct/reliable information both in the
municipality’s financial position and financial performance. Henceforth, the
Municipal Accountant should set up a trust liability account in the trust fund
book for any unspent DRRMF of the next preceding year.

3.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. check prior and current years’ accounting entries in the journals and
prepare necessary adjusting entries both in Trust Fund and General
Fund books following the pro-forma accounting entries provided;
b. ensure that recording of equipment and the like is transferred to the
General Fund book, and the provision of its depreciation is done in
the GF book;
c. submit to COA the adjusting entries prepared for further evaluation,
as well as the adjusted Financial Statements, both of Trust Fund and
the Combined FS; and
d. provide and submit a schedule, as of December 31, 2019, of fund
transfers from National Government Agencies after adjustments.

4.8 We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Budget
Officer observe proper procedure in charging expenditures to the respective
budgets for each particular year and for the Municipal Accountant to record
items of expenses in the appropriate accounting records of the period to which
they relate.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed to strictly observe the 2%
limitation provided by the law on the appropriate utilization of expenses
involving extraordinary and miscellaneous items.

6.7 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, along with its
Secretariat, judiciously observe the requirements on proper posting prescribed
by the Revised IRR of RA 9184, in order to ensure that the principle of
transparency and competitiveness is substantially achieved in its procurement
activities.

7.5 We recommended that management submit explanation and/or
justification letter to provide clarification on the circumstances and reasons for
the incurrence of the delay in the payment of regular amortizations on the loan
undertaken.

8.6 We recommend that the Chairperson of the GFPS TWG be required to
submit GAD Plan and Budget and its Accomplishment Report for CY 2019.
Henceforth, the same should be required to strictly adhere to Item V of COA
Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA
audit team.

9.7 We recommended and management agreed that the MDRRMO and the
Municipal Accountant submit to COA the Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF for CY 2019 using the latest prescribed format. Henceforth, the same
employees should strictly adhere to the reporting requirements of the pertinent
guidelines by submitting the Monthly and Annual Reports on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF, as well as the LDRRMFIP, within the time frame.

We recommend that management requires offices/departments to submit a list of all properties assigned to them to facilitate the conduct and submission of inventory reports and for the inventory team to easily identify existing assets. Likewise, require the Inventory Committee to expedite the immediate completion of the Year-End Inventory Report.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to prepare and submit Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) where all unserviceable properties are reported to in preparation for the disposal.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report not submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Submitted such documents but only for the procurements of goods and services through bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions mandated in Sections 7 and 48 of the IRR of RA 9184 and discontinue the process of paying for procured goods and services through reimbursement.
We recommend that management install signboards in the design and format prescribed by COA Circular No. 2013-004 for those infrastructure projects implemented by administration.
We recommend that management through its MSWDO to submit the above set of documents so that a proper and thorough audit and evaluation of the ESA Fund releases can be made.
observations.managementAction: Management through MSWDO to submit the above set of documents so that a proper and thorough audit and evaluation of the ESA Fund releases can be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit immediately the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2015 duly certified by the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, they should submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. He should request the member of the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations so that the Solid Waste Management Program can be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The management made an initial step such as for the alternative technology and the enclosure of the said dumpsite perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider the filling-up of vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the LGU for the benefit of the constituents. Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the municipality of its functions, targets and programs.
observations.managementAction: Management should prioritize and consider the filling-up of vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the LGU for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason: Some vacant position are still unfilled because of the absence of qualified applicant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions mandated in Section 7 and 48 of the IRR of RA 9184 and discontinue the process of paying for procured goods and services through reimbursements.

The Municipal Mayor should strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and disapprove the grant of additional cash advance to all officers and employees who have outstanding balances in their cash advances. He should instruct the Accountant to refrain from processing new cash advances to officers and employees who have failed to liquidate their previous cash advances, and cause the withholding of the accountable officers’ salaries equivalent to the unliquidated amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant send demand letters to officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should direct the driver and/or employee concerned to strictly comply with the Manual of Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-66. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the documentary requirements are submitted first before processing payments, which shall serve as basis for recording gasoline consumption.

The Municipal Mayor should create an Inventory Team to conduct a periodic physical inventory of property in accordance with Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I, to be able to submit the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), which should be reconciled with the accounting records. A timeline to finish the inventory should be established.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management created an Inventory Team and conducting periodic physical inventory; a timeline to finish the inventory should be established.
